findings.message: `extract_variant_payload_types()` is lossy for rustdoc variants with stripped payload fields: `Tuple(Vec<Option<Id>>)` drops `None` entries via `filter_map`, and `Struct { has_stripped_fields: true }` is ignored entirely. That means a variant with hidden/stripped fields is exported with a shorter `payload_types` list than the source actually has, silently corrupting the schema instead of failing closed.

The struct-variant branch in `extract_variant_payload_types()` still silently skips missing or non-`StructField` ids (`continue` at the end of the loop), so a malformed or unexpectedly pruned rustdoc entry can produce a truncated `payload_types` list without surfacing an error. That breaks the fail-closed behavior described in the comment and can export incomplete enum metadata. See [libs/infrastructure/src/schema_export.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/infrastructure/src/schema_export.rs#L491).

The new variant DTOs still build `EnumVariantDeclaration` through infallible constructors on decode, so JSON can supply `"name": ""` and the codecs will accept it as an invalid domain value. The domain type already exposes `try_new()` for the non-empty-name invariant, so the boundary should reject empty variant names instead of normalizing them into the domain. See [libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/catalogue_codec.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/catalogue_codec.rs#L1047) and [libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/baseline_codec.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/infrastructure/src/tddd/baseline_codec.rs#L269).

These fixtures still use the old `expected_variants: ["A", "B"]` shape. With the new `EnumVariantDeclarationDto` schema, serde fails before the custom `InvalidEntry` path, so `decode()` now returns `Json` and these assertions fail. The same issue appears again at lines 2782 and 3612.

`extract_enum_variants()` silently skips enum variant entries when `krate.index` has no item or the item has no name. That turns a malformed/partial rustdoc export into an incomplete `SchemaExport` instead of failing closed, even though this helper already treats stripped payload fields as fatal. Public enums should not lose variants silently.

`member_from_dto()` accepts `Variant { name, payload_types }` but never validates `payload_types` the way the other codecs do. A baseline JSON entry can therefore contain module-qualified payload types like `domain::Foo`, decode successfully, and then round-trip into an L1 baseline even though the rest of the infrastructure rejects `::` in type strings. Add the same validation before constructing `EnumVariantDeclaration`.

`expected_members` variant decoding still constructs `MemberDeclaration::variant` after only checking for `::`, bypassing the new `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new` validation that rejects empty, whitespace, and non-identifier variant names. A catalogue JSON entry with `{"kind":"variant","name":" ","payload_types":[]}` is accepted into an invalid domain value, while the `expected_variants` path in this same file correctly fails closed. Route this branch through `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new` and map the error to `InvalidEntry`.

The ADR requires `payload_types` entries to be complete type strings, including generic arguments, but both renderer sections describe resolving each `payload_types` value as a type token through `type_index`. For payloads like `Result<ProposedDecision, DomainError>` or `Vec<ProposedDecision>`, the declared value will not match a catalogue type name directly, so the planned edge generation would miss the inner payload types while still requiring authors to write the generic-complete form. Clarify that renderers must extract type-name tokens from each payload type string before resolving, or constrain `payload_types` to catalogue type names only.

`T002` and `T003` both suppress `dst_id == src_id` / self-loops for variant payload edges. That drops legitimate recursive enum payloads, so cases like `enum Node { Next(Node) }` or `Box<Node>` will lose a real enum→payload edge in both Contract Map and Reality View. The new feature therefore cannot represent one of the most important payload shapes it is supposed to expose.

`T002` and `T003` suppress variant-payload self-loops, but the spec requires enum -> payload type edges for resolved payload types. Recursive payloads such as `enum Node { Next(Box<Node>) }` resolve back to the enum itself, so the plan would drop a legitimate payload edge in both renderers and fail the stated representation semantics. Remove that suppression for variant-payload edges or explicitly exclude recursive payloads from the spec.

`OS-03` cites `knowledge/adr/2026-04-28-0135-tddd-struct-kind-uniformization-and-catalogue-linter.md#OS-04`, but the referenced ADR does not define an `OS-04` design decision/section for this rationale. The out-of-scope boundary is therefore not traceable from the SSoT citation.

`EnumVariantDeclaration` is described by the spec and implementation plan as a new value object to add, but its catalogue entry has no `action` field, so the generated view renders the action as `—` instead of `add`. That contradicts T001/IN-01 and weakens the plan's signal semantics for this new type; declare it with `"action": "add"` to match the rest of the plan-artifact intent.

`build_type_graph` is described as both consuming a `SchemaExport` and extracting payload data from rustdoc JSON. The implementation plan assigns the actual rustdoc extraction to `schema_export.rs::extract_enum_variants()`, so this catalogue entry/spec reference puts the responsibility on the wrong component and leaves IN-07/AC-02 semantically inconsistent for implementers.

The plan says `variant_payload` should render with `"-->"` as the same arrowhead as a field edge, and CN-04 also documents field edges as `-->|.field_name|`. Existing TypeGraph field-edge semantics are solid no-arrow containment (`---|field_name|`), so following this plan would make the new Reality View variant edge inconsistent with the stated field-edge comparison and likely change the intended visual semantics.

IN-07 assigns rustdoc JSON payload extraction to `build_type_graph`, but the implementation plan assigns that extraction to `schema_export.rs::extract_enum_variants()` and says `build_type_graph` merely receives payload-aware declarations; `infrastructure-types.json` likewise says `build_type_graph` propagates `SchemaExport` values without re-parsing rustdoc JSON. This leaves the responsibility boundary contradictory for T001/AC-02/CN-06.

CN-04 describes the normal field edge as directed (`-->|.field_name|`), and the S3 narrative says `variant_payload` should use the same `-->` arrowhead as field edges, but T003 then says the new arm is `-->|label|` “distinct from field edges which use `---|label|`”. The plan is internally inconsistent about whether Reality View field edges are directed or undirected, so an implementer cannot tell which edge semantics to preserve.

The `MemberDeclaration` entry still says `expected_variants` are listed in pre-migration string form, but the regenerated artifact immediately below is already using object-form variants with `name` and `payload_types`. That prose now contradicts the schema this file is documenting.

The `MemberDeclarationDto` description mixes the legacy and migrated Variant shapes (`Variant { name, payload_types: String } (old bare-name)`), which is internally inconsistent. If the old schema was a bare-name arm, this should be rewritten so the migration history matches the actual schema shape being described.

`domain-types.json` mixes old and new schema narration for `MemberDeclaration`: the description at line 37 says the `expected_variants` are still in pre-migration string form, but the actual JSON immediately below is already the structured `EnumVariantDeclaration` shape. That contradiction will confuse readers about which schema the artifact currently represents.

`infrastructure-types.json` misstates the pre-migration `MemberDeclarationDto::Variant` shape at line 52. The note describes the old form as an object with `name` and `payload_types`, but the earlier schema was the bare `Variant(String)` form. This is a factual error in the migration note, not just wording.

`domain-types.md` renders the `Signal` column as `—` for every row, but the source signal file still marks these entries as blue/yellow. For example, `EnumVariantDeclaration` is yellow and `render_contract_map` is yellow in `domain-type-signals.json`, while the rendered table at [domain-types.md](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/enum-variant-payload-schema-2026-05-02/domain-types.md:7) hides that state. This makes the generated view inconsistent with its own signal data.

`infrastructure-types.md` has the same mismatch: the rendered `Signal` column is `—` for `BaselineCodecError`, `MemberDeclarationDto`, and the renderer functions, but `infrastructure-type-signals.json` still marks the first two blue and the three free functions yellow. The rendered table at [infrastructure-types.md](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/enum-variant-payload-schema-2026-05-02/infrastructure-types.md:7) no longer matches the signal source.

T001 is now marked complete in `plan.md`, but T001-owned artifacts still report unresolved signals: `EnumVariantDeclaration` remains 🟡 in `domain-types.md` and `build_type_graph`, whose description ties it to T001/IN-07, remains 🟡 in `infrastructure-types.md`. The accepted residual yellow state is for later T002/T003 renderer work, so the plan artifacts now contradict themselves about whether T001 is actually done.

`EnumVariantDeclaration::new` and `MemberDeclaration::variant` are unchecked pass-through constructors, so the new public enum-variant value object still allows invalid states in-memory (for example empty/whitespace names or payload strings that only get rejected later by codec validation). That weakens the new domain invariant, and there is no direct test coverage for the payload-bearing path to catch regressions.

Public construction still bypasses the new enum-variant name validation. `EnumVariantDeclaration::new` is public, and `MemberDeclaration::variant` / `unit_variant` call it directly, so empty or whitespace-only variant names can still enter the domain model even though `SpecValidationError::EmptyVariantName` was added.

`EnumVariantDeclaration` still allows impossible variant names to enter the domain model. `try_new` only rejects blank/whitespace names, and the public unchecked constructors (`new`, plus `MemberDeclaration::variant/unit_variant`) accept any string, so values like `"Foo Bar"` or `"Foo::Bar"` can be constructed even though they cannot be real Rust variants. That weakens the new invariant and lets invalid catalogue state leak into later evaluation/serialization paths.

`EnumVariantDeclaration` still has a public unchecked constructor, and `MemberDeclaration::variant/unit_variant` both call it directly. That means callers can bypass the new validation entirely and still build `TypeDefinitionKind::{Enum,ErrorType}` entries with empty or malformed variant names, so the invariant is not actually enforced at the domain boundary.

`try_new`'s identifier predicate accepts `_` as a valid variant name, but `_` is not a legal Rust enum variant identifier. As written, `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new("_", vec![])` will succeed and admit an impossible state.

`EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new` only enforces an ASCII identifier regex, so reserved words like `fn` or `type` still pass even though they are not legal enum variants. The public `new`/`MemberDeclaration::variant`/`unit_variant` constructors also bypass validation entirely, so the new variant-name invariant is still easy to violate from domain code.

P1: The new `payload_types` field is never validated in enum forward checks. `evaluate_enum` and `evaluate_error_type` collapse both spec and code to `name()` sets only, so `Foo(String)` and `Foo(u32)` still match as Blue. That means the payload-schema change is not actually enforced by the domain evaluator. See [signals.rs:439](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/domain/src/tddd/signals.rs#L439) and [signals.rs:534](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/domain/src/tddd/signals.rs#L534).

P1: The new invariant is still bypassable through public unchecked constructors. `EnumVariantDeclaration::new` is public, and `MemberDeclaration::variant` / `unit_variant` call it directly, so callers can still manufacture invalid variant names without ever hitting `try_new`. That leaves the domain type able to hold illegal states again. See [catalogue.rs:280](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue.rs#L280) and [catalogue.rs:330](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/libs/domain/src/tddd/catalogue.rs#L330).

The new enum-variant invariant is still not enforced at the domain boundary. `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new()` only validates `name`, while the public `new()` constructor and `MemberDeclaration::variant()/unit_variant()` remain infallible and bypass validation entirely, so callers can still construct `EnumVariantDeclaration`s with invalid names or arbitrary `payload_types` strings. That leaves `TypeDefinitionKind::Enum` / `ErrorType` able to carry illegal states, and the new payload-aware signal checks will compare unchecked strings instead of validated L1 type names.

P1: The new enum-variant validation is bypassable in the public API. `EnumVariantDeclaration::new` is unchecked, and both `MemberDeclaration::variant` / `unit_variant` call it directly, so callers can still construct illegal variant names or payload types and carry them into catalogue/baseline/rendering. That defeats the new invariant the patch is trying to enforce; the checked path needs to be the default or the unchecked path should not be public.

P1: `RUST_KEYWORDS` is incomplete: `union` is a Rust keyword but is not listed, so `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new("union", vec![])` currently succeeds even though the name cannot be emitted as a normal enum variant. The checked constructor therefore still accepts an illegal state.

P1: The new enum-variant invariant is still bypassable from the public API. `EnumVariantDeclaration::new` is unchecked, and `MemberDeclaration::variant` / `unit_variant` call it directly, so callers can still manufacture invalid variant names or payloads without ever hitting `try_new`. That leaves `TypeDefinitionKind::Enum` / `ErrorType` able to carry illegal states in-memory.

P1: `EnumVariantDeclaration::try_new` does not actually validate payload type strings beyond rejecting `::`. Empty or whitespace-only entries in `payload_types` still pass, even though they are not valid type names and will later be treated as real variant payloads. The checked constructor therefore still admits invalid enum metadata.

P1: `EnumVariantDeclaration::new` is public and `MemberDeclaration::variant`/`unit_variant` call it directly, so callers can still construct invalid enum variants without going through the checked constructor. That leaves the new variant-name/payload invariants bypassable at the domain boundary.

P1: `try_new` still accepts payload strings with surrounding whitespace because it only rejects blank strings and `::`. A value like `" UserId "` passes validation even though it is not a canonical L1 type string and will later fail to round-trip or match code reality.

`MemberDeclaration::variant` and `unit_variant` still wrap `EnumVariantDeclaration::new`, which skips both name and payload validation. That means callers can still construct invalid enum variants and bypass the new `SpecValidationError` checks, so the domain model still admits illegal enum states.
